features.reason: Integrity/lint gate: cargo fmt --check exits 1 on the landed integration tip — formatting drift in cli/src/commands/hooks_list.rs (BINDING_KEY_ORDER and RECORDED_BINDING_ORDER const layout collapsed to single-line by rustfmt vs the multi-line form committed) and cli/src/settings.rs (the worktree_setup let-binding in a test). This project's own bound `lint` hook (confirmed via `the-loop hooks-list`) is `npm run check && cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --all-targets`, and prior rust-* feature validation procedures (rust-crate-scaffold, plan-commands-rust) treat cargo fmt --check as part of the mandatory lint gate. The drift is confirmed present on loop/run-commands-rust--hooks-drift-parity in isolation (checked out standalone, unmerged) and confirmed absent on the rust-replatform base branch, so it is a genuine defect introduced by that branch's own commits, not a merge-assembly artifact.; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, npm test (383/383), npm run lint (eslint), and the Rust-target oracle corpus (54 pass / 0 fail / 0 pending) are all green on the fully committed integration tip — only the cargo fmt gate fails.; Acceptance criteria 1-5's behavioral surfaces are otherwise well evidenced: the executor's fixture-repo exercises (worktree-create/idempotent/remove/cwd-inside-refusal/bound-provisioning-success/bound-provisioning-teardown-on-failure, calibration-summarize happy+malformed, hooks-list --compact byte match against the JS CLI including worktreeSetup) and the full green rust-target oracle corpus back criteria 1-5, recorded in docs/validation/run-commands-rust/procedure.md on the (now-discarded, but branch-preserved) integrate--run-commands-rust branch — this is not what is blocking the pass.
